---
title: "Phase 5c — npixels sweep (single-skycell case study)"
subtitle: "The size-above-threshold floor is the binding constraint for point sources"
---

::: {.callout-note}
Same scope note as [Phase 5b](phase5b_kernel_sweep.qmd): this page
shows the npixels sweep on one validation skycell. The Phase 6
summary confirms the finding across all 10 mosaics (npixels=16
gives +0.5 mag for stars at every depth).
:::

```{python}
#| echo: false
from pathlib import Path
import pandas as pd
REPO = Path("../..").resolve()
```

## What Phase 5c does

Rerun `SourceCatalogStep` on the same Phase-4a mosaic with
`npixels ∈ {9, 16, 25}`, keeping `kernel_fwhm = 2.0` (near-optimal
from [Phase 5b](phase5b_kernel_sweep.qmd)) and the other defaults.
`npixels` is the minimum *connected pixels above threshold* required
to register as a detection — 25 is the MosaicPipeline default.

## Results

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| tbl-cap: "npixels sweep summary at kernel_fwhm=2."
summary = pd.read_csv(REPO / "output/detection/phase5c/sweep_summary_pair_11119.csv")
summary
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "LEFT: star detection efficiency vs input F158 mag, for npixels 9/16/25. RIGHT: same for galaxies. Default (yellow, npixels=25) is strictly worse than the lower values at every magnitude. At npixels=9, stars remain at 100% completeness out to mag 26 (the limit of our injection grid)."
from IPython.display import Image, display
display(Image(filename=str(REPO / "output/detection/phase5c/sweep_pair_11119.png")))
```

## Stars see a huge benefit, galaxies much smaller

| npixels | stars m50% | Δ vs default | galaxies m50% | Δ vs default | FP rate |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 9 | ≥26.0 (saturates above mag range) | ≥ +0.6 | 24.88 | +0.23 | 1.4% |
| 16 | 25.89 | +0.49 | 24.87 | +0.22 | 0.3% |
| **25 (default)** | **25.40** | — | **24.65** | — | 0.0% |

The default `npixels=25` is the binding constraint for *point sources*
at our depth. A Roman F158 PSF on a 0.055″ drizzled mosaic has FWHM
~2.7 pix. After `kernel_fwhm=2` convolution the core extends over ~15
pixels above 3σ at mag 23, shrinking to ~20-25 at mag 25, and dropping
below 25 for fainter stars — so the 25-pixel floor simply cuts off
the faint-end tail.

Galaxies (Sersic n=1, r_eff=0.275″) have intrinsic brightness profiles
that spread over more pixels at any magnitude, so `npixels=25` is
already well below the typical galaxy footprint — dropping it only
gains a little.

## False-positive caveat

At npixels=9 we see 5 FPs out of 345 (1.4%), up from 0 at npixels=25.
Our truth density is ~0.4 sources per arcsec² (420 in a ~1000 arcsec²
skycell core), so a random position has ~3% chance of being within
0.3″ of a real truth source by chance. Some of those 5 "FPs" may
therefore be genuine noise peaks, some may be truth matches lost in
attribution. At npixels=16 we see 1 FP (0.3%) — comfortably below
that chance-coincidence rate, so likely a real false detection.

## Takeaway for the detection study

For the depth-10 HLWAS mosaics this study produces, **npixels is the
binding parameter, not kernel_fwhm**. The "kernel_fwhm helps extended
sources" design assumption misses the real optimization axis.

Recommendation for Phase 4b/5 at scale: use
**`kernel_fwhm=2`, `npixels=16`** — gains 0.5 mag star depth / 0.2
mag galaxy depth with only 0.3% FP rate, a marginal cost. Dropping
to `npixels=9` gains a little more but adds ambiguity at the FP floor.

## Checkpoint

Parameter studies on the single validation mosaic complete. Default
`kernel_fwhm=2` stays; npixels moves from 25 to 16. Ready for the
skycell-driven Phase 1-2 refactor and Phase 4b scale-up.
